column1: # ZAP API Scan rules — suppress known false positives for pitchey-api-prod.
# Format: <rule-id>\t<IGNORE|WARN|FAIL>\t<comment>
# Reference: https://www.zaproxy.org/docs/automate/automation-framework/
# Every rule listed here was observed as a false positive in issue #41
# (2026-04-22 ZAP API Scan Report). Details: issue #37.
# IMPORTANT: adding a rule here only silences ZAP's classification of that
# alert as failing. New rule IDs not in this list still surface normally.
# Review periodically — do not grow this list to hide real findings.
# ZAP probes `/computeMetadata/v1/`, `/latest/meta-data/`, `/metadata/instance`,
# `/metadata/v1`, `/opc/v1/instance/` — paths the Worker correctly 404s.
# Returning 4xx JSON for a cloud-metadata path is the intended behavior;
# ZAP flags the response Content-Type mismatch as "unexpected."

column3: Unexpected Content-Type on deliberate 4xx JSON responses to cloud-metadata probes
A Client Error response is correct behavior for unauth/missing routes

column1: # `pitchey-session` is a UUID v4 cookie. Base64-decodable by coincidence of
# UUID's character set; not an encoded secret.

column3: pitchey-session UUID cookie matched as Base64 by pattern heuristic

column1: # Sec-Fetch-Dest / Mode / Site / User are REQUEST headers the browser sends
# to the server, not response headers. ZAP is flagging that ZAP's own scanner
# didn't send them. Not actionable by the server.

column3: Sec-Fetch-* are request headers (client-sent), not server response headers

column1: # Root / is public marketing content; cacheability is intentional.

column3: Storable/cacheable public content is intentional on marketing routes
